response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins by releasing histamines which cause allergic reactions that can be severe or life threatening when exposed to peanuts or peanut products  
this reaction is not related to the environment of living underwater but rather to the presence of allergens in their surroundings which may be found in food sources or other materials they come into contact with  

Sec. 2  
living underwater would require a completely different set of conditions and resources that are not compatible with the human body's need for oxygen and the typical diet of a person with a peanut allergy  
therefore the inability to live underwater is not due to the allergy itself but due to the fundamental biological limitations of human survival in an aquatic environment regardless of allergies
